articles.title: Clop ransomware targets Windchill, FlexPLM in data theft attacks
articles.description: The Clop ransomware gang (also tracked as Cl0p) is targeting Internet-exposed PTC Windchill and FlexPLM instances in a new data theft extortion campaign. [...]

articles.title: New Dolphin X malware uses AI to rank high-value targets
articles.description: A new Dolphin X remote access trojan claims to use an AI-powered profiling feature to score and rank infected users, helping cybercriminals identify which victims should be targeted first. [...]

articles.title: Fake Claude app promoted by Bing ads pushes SectopRAT malware

articles.title: Russian hackers exploit Zimbra zero-click flaw for email theft

articles.title: FedRAMP Rev5 Is Ending: What the 20x Transition Really Requires
articles.description: FedRAMP 20X replaces point-in-time assessments with continuous, machine-readable evidence that demonstrates security controls are working. Anecdotes explains what the transition from Rev5 to FedRAMP 20X means and how organizations can prepare for continuous, evidence-based assurance. [...]

articles.title: New RefluXFS Linux flaw lets attackers gain root privileges
articles.description: A nine-year-old race condition vulnerability in the Linux kernel's XFS filesystem, tracked as CVE-2026-64600, allows local attackers to overwrite protected files and gain root privileges. [...]

articles.title: How enterprise GenAI can amplify ransomware risk — and how to contain it
